mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by > 0.1pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 9.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 1mo, or t-test p > 0.05 for return differences in high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise > 1% YoY, or interaction term coefficient < 0.1 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO rises > 1.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) increase discount rates for equity cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or t-test p > 0.1 for return differences in high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 130k signals weak labor market performance that reduces consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS falls below 130k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST and RSAFS both rise > 2% MoM, or interaction term coefficient < 0.15 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines > 1.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
